description: Code quality fixes across side panel components: consistent cn() usage, Input cleanup, remove dead code (Loader asChild, AccordionHeader), fix Text export style.

userStories.title: Use cn() consistently in Input and NumberStepper instead of template literals
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Input and NumberStepper retro primitives to use cn() for className composition so that consumer-provided classes can correctly override base styles via tailwind-merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Input.tsx uses cn() from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/lib/cn.ts for composing its className
Input.tsx explicitly destructures aria-invalid and uses cn() for the conditional destructive styling
NumberStepper.tsx uses cn() for composing its outer container className
Both components correctly handle consumer className overrides (tailwind-merge resolves conflicts)
Input.stories.tsx and NumberStepper.stories.tsx still render correctly

userStories.title: Remove unused asChild from Loader and dead AccordionHeader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dead code removed from the retro primitives so the API surface is clean and accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Loader.tsx no longer has asChild in its LoaderProps interface
The AccordionHeader sub-component is removed from Accordion.tsx (the Object.assign no longer includes Header)
No consumer references AccordionHeader or Loader asChild
Storybook stories for Accordion and Loader still work correctly
userStories.notes:
platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Loader.tsx:26 declares asChild in LoaderProps but it is never used in the component body — it falls into ...props and becomes a meaningless DOM attribute. Remove it from the interface.

platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx exports Accordion.Header (AccordionHeader sub-component at lines 23-40), but no consumer uses it. PluginCard.tsx builds its own header using AccordionPrimitive.Header and AccordionPrimitive.Trigger directly because it needs a custom layout with a switch button outside the trigger. Remove AccordionHeader and its entry in the Object.assign on line 58. Also remove the ChevronDown import if it becomes unused after removing AccordionHeader. Check Accordion.stories.tsx — if any story uses Accordion.Header, update it to not use the removed sub-component.

userStories.title: Fix Text export style and add PluginCard comment about raw Radix usage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent export patterns and clear documentation of intentional deviations from the component library.
acceptanceCriteria: Text.tsx uses 'export { Text }' pattern (named export declaration at the bottom) instead of inline 'export const Text'
PluginCard.tsx has a brief comment explaining why AccordionPrimitive.Header and AccordionPrimitive.Trigger are used directly instead of the retro Accordion wrapper
userStories.notes:
platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Text.tsx:29 uses 'export const Text = ...' while every other retro component uses the pattern of defining the component and then exporting via 'export { ComponentName as Name }' or 'export { Name }'. Change Text to match: define as 'const Text = ...' and export at the bottom as 'export { Text }'.

In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx, around lines 9 and 65-66, the component imports AccordionPrimitive directly alongside the retro Accordion. Add a one-line comment above the import explaining: the PluginCard needs a custom accordion header layout (icon + name + switch outside the trigger area) that the retro AccordionHeader does not support.
